refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize energy consumption per ton moved to meet or exceed the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the high-level FR into a measurable and actionable sub-FR focused on energy efficiency KPI enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure total energy used and tons moved over the evaluation period and calculate energy_per_ton to verify it meets ≤ E_target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor and report energy consumption accurately.
FR2: Optimize vehicle operational parameters to reduce energy usage.
FR3: Provide feedback and control to operators to maintain energy efficiency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Integrate energy metering sensors and telemetry for real-time monitoring.
DP2: Implement adaptive vehicle control algorithms to optimize fuel/electricity usage.
DP3: Develop operator assistance interface with energy efficiency feedback.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Integrate energy metering sensors and telemetry for real-time monitoring.

potential_sources: Telemetry data dependency between energy metering and operator feedback modules.
Vehicle control algorithms coupling with energy sensors.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically supports FR1 and enables data flow to other DPs with manageable coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate and reliable energy sensors are available.
Risk of sensor data latency affecting real-time feedback.
Assumes telemetry network link is stable and secure.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 in subsequent decomposition levels to address optimization and operator feedback.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical components including energy sensors and onboard telemetry systems.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmit energy consumption data to NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Support data communication between Vehicle and remote modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as energy sensors and telemetry hardware are physically part of Vehicle.
Receives telemetry data but does not contain physical sensors.
Uses telemetry data for remote control but no direct containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy metering and telemetry hardware logically belong within the Vehicle module to minimize interface complexity and maximize data fidelity.
containment_risks: Potential hardware integration complexity within Vehicle.
Dependency on stable network link for telemetry data transmission.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate telemetry data handling in NGHE Solution module to ensure efficient data processing and minimize diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (energy metering and telemetry) directly addresses the refined FR of monitoring energy consumption, enabling measurable verification.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry data diffusion across multiple modules may increase interface complexity.
Network link reliability impacts data availability.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Assigning DP1 to Vehicle module ensures physical containment of sensors, reducing diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Energy consumption data can be verified via telemetry outputs from Vehicle module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership of energy monitoring hardware and data transmission within Vehicle module.

recommended_revisions: Plan subsequent decomposition for optimization and operator feedback DPs.
Review telemetry data handling in NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules to reduce diffusion.

decision.justification: While the initial decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement is needed to incorporate optimization and operator feedback DPs and their IM assignments to fully address the energy efficiency KPI.
next_level_sub_frs: FR2: Optimize vehicle operational parameters to reduce energy usage.
FR3: Provide feedback and control to operators to maintain energy efficiency.

revise.why: To include additional sub-FRs and corresponding DPs and IMs that address optimization and operator feedback, ensuring comprehensive coverage of the energy efficiency requirement.

assumptions: Energy sensors and telemetry hardware are physically integrated into Vehicle.
Telemetry and network links are reliable for data transmission.
open_risks: Potential latency and reliability issues in telemetry data affecting real-time feedback.
evidence_needed: Sensor accuracy and reliability test results.
